EN.title: Command-Line Tool (vw)
EN.description: Centralized PowerShell automation entrypoint and utility script router for VaultWares
EN.body:
# Command-Line Tool (`vw`)

To streamline and unify the vast collection of PowerShell automation, synchronization, and media-processing scripts across the VaultWares workspace, all useful utilities have been consolidated under a single command-line interface called `vw`.

The `vw` utility operates globally, routing parameters down to modular scripts located in various repositories across the workspace.

---

## Global Access

The CLI is exposed as the global `vw` command. It is implemented as a batch wrapper (`vw.bat`) pointing to the PowerShell router (`vw.ps1`).

```bash
vw <command> [arguments]
```

To view the main help menu, run:

```bash
vw --help
```

---

## Command Registry

Commands are grouped into logical operational categories:

### Workspace Management
- **`sync`**: Run bulk Git pull and workspace synchronization across repository layers.
- **`shortcuts`**: Install or refresh Desktop shortcuts for standard tool configurations.
- **`rebuild`**: Clean and rebuild python packages, node modules, and dependencies.
- **`recreate-venvs`**: Reinitialize virtual environments (`.venv`) across workspaces.
- **`launch-team`**: Dispatch team of supervisor and worker AI agents.
- **`deploy-dispatch`**: Bootstrap VaultWares dispatch server directories.
- **`backup-task`**: Create a scheduled task to run the Auto Backup script silently every 5 minutes.
- **`swap-sfw`**: Toggle and hide/unhide directories based on IsSFW environment variable.
- **`find-scripts`**: Search workspace and output script file listings to the Desktop.

### Git Automation
- **`git-pull-all`**: Iterate through workspace and pull updates for submodules and branches.
- **`git-auto`**: Execute repository checks and send email alerts on remote changes.

### Agent Ledger
- **`archive-ledger`**: Archive historical ledger entries to optimize performance.
- **`record-change`**: Log an agent transaction or changes to the Shared Agent Ledger database.
- **`render-ledger`**: Generate detailed HTML/Markdown reports of agent activities.
- **`render-impact`**: Compile work metrics and update visual workspace progression indicators.
- **`sync-ledger`**: Sync local ledger records to central database endpoints.
- **`start-tracker`**: Launch the keyboard/mouse idle telemetry service in the background.

#### Input Tracker restart policy

Operational note, Thu, 02 Jul 2026 16:45: the Input Tracker is a resident Python process (`agent-ledger/scripts/track-input.py`). Updating or pulling `agent-ledger` does not reload that process by itself, so an old tracker can keep running stale code even when the scheduled task appears healthy.

The durable fix is to run the tracker through `agent-ledger/scripts/start-input-tracker.ps1` and register `VaultWares-InputTracker` with `MultipleInstances = Parallel`. The task action must be `conhost.exe --headless "pwsh.exe" ... start-input-tracker.ps1`; this is the proven stable launch mode for the resident tracker. Do not simplify it back to plain `powershell.exe`, visible `pwsh.exe`, or a direct Python task when repairing crashes.

Operational note, Thu, 02 Jul 2026 18:06: the launcher must only stop old `track-input.py` Python child processes for the same script path. It must not match `start-input-tracker.ps1` wrappers, because the headless task's parent `conhost.exe` command line contains the launcher path; killing that wrapper makes Task Scheduler report a crash such as `0xffffffff`. A healthy restart leaves Task Scheduler at `267009` while the new resident tracker is running.

After any tracker code change or repo sync, verify with:

```powershell
Start-ScheduledTask -TaskName "VaultWares-InputTracker"
Start-Sleep -Seconds 70
Get-Content "C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\Github Repos\agent-ledger\input-state\input-tracker-health.json"
```

The health payload should show a fresh `updated_at`, a current `pid`, `status` as `online` or `listening`, and a `last_post_at` after the restart once activity has flushed.

### System Monitoring
- **`health-alarm`**: Start the telemetry status alert service using local environment configuration.
- **`health-probe`**: Run immediate ping checks and log network reachability for all nodes.
- **`pg-watchdog`**: Launch background monitor to auto-restart Postgres service if hung.
- **`drill-kill-pg`**: Forcefully close active Postgres database connections and clean locks.
- **`test-alarm`**: Dispatch simulated SMTP notification message for alert verification.
- **`audit-menu`**: Audit Windows Explorer shell context menu handler registry keys.
- **`list-thirdparty`**: Identify custom/third-party executables and PowerShell commands in session.
- **`list-programs`**: Query local registry hives and remote connections for installed software.
- **`kill-cowork`**: Stop the CoworkVMService windows service.

### Media Pipeline
- **`move-videos`**: Move all video files from subfolders recursively to the root directory.
- **`unzip-dedupe`**: Decompress volumes and delete matching hash duplicates automatically.
- **`upscale-test`**: Execute standalone upscaling test using local PyTorch/Real-ESRGAN dependencies.
- **`telegram-pipeline`**: Execute telethon link resolver pipeline with system notifications.
- **`auto-sync`**: Align external audio with a target video by waveform correlation and fingerprinting.
- **`manual-sync`**: Offset video or audio tracks by a specified millisecond value using FFmpeg.
- **`fix-audio-subs`**: Separate and normalize voice tracks, mix with background audio, and encode via CUDA.

### Backup & Recovery
- **`auto-backup`**: Maintain two backup slots (latest and daily) across primary and secondary storage.

### Service Management
- **`install-service-mcp`**: Install and start the VaultWares MCP Server background service.
- **`install-service-realtime`**: Install and start the Vaultwares Realtime background service.
- **`install-service-central`**: Install and start the VaultCentral background service.
- **`install-service-automation`**: Install and start the Automation Suite background service.
- **`install-service-transcriber`**: Install and start the Video Transcriber background service.
- **`install-service-weeklymenu`**: Install and start the Weekly Menu background service.

### Vault Explorer
- **`benchmark-asr`**: Measure speech-to-text benchmark performance using local Whisper models.
- **`audio-normalize`**: Run loudness normalization package recursively on video/audio folders.
- **`imagemagick-enhance`**: Enhance cover artwork/thumbnails using ImageMagick filters.
- **`livestream-translator`**: Translate spoken audio tracks from RTMP/HTTP livestreams in real-time.
- **`preview-generator`**: Compile preview hover-grids, thumbnails, and contact sheets.
- **`realesrgan-enhance`**: Upscale thumbnail assets using PyTorch CUDA Real-ESRGAN.
- **`rtx-vsr`**: Render video upscales using NVIDIA RTX Video Super Resolution.
- **`subtitles`**: Generate SRT/VTT subtitles from spoken audio using speech models.
- **`thumb-enhance`**: Optimize cover pages and preview layouts.
- **`translate-spoken`**: Recursively translate spoken dialog in video files via Whisper/TTS.
- **`stream-translator`**: Run Stream spoken translation for active HTTPS audio/video streams.

### Security & Server
- **`generate-certs`**: Generate local dev SSL certificates using mkcert.
- **`start-https`**: Spin up local front-end Astro development server under HTTPS.
- **`build-gradio`**: Compile python Gradio application using PyInstaller standalone builder.
- **`distribute-secrets`**: Rotate and distribute `GH_TOKEN` and `JIRA_TOKEN` secrets to repositories.

### Corporate Standards
- **`sync-instructions`**: Propagate global system prompts and rules across all repositories.
- **`sync-skills`**: Sync global assistant skills files to local repo locations.
- **`validate-protocols`**: Validate repo system instruction formatting and metadata compliance.

### USD Studio
- **`download-colmap`**: Download portable COLMAP 3D reconstruction tool.
- **`setup-usd`**: Bootstrap PyTorch and CUDA workspace for USD Digital Twin Playground.
- **`build-worker-img`**: Build and push docker worker containers.
- **`build-demo-exe`**: Compile standalone USD Studio Demo executable.

---

## Addition Protocol

To extend the global `vw` CLI with a new automation script, follow these standard steps:

<Steps>
  <Step title="Place the script in the workspace">
    Save the script in the most relevant sub-repository (e.g. `vault-explorer/scripts/pwsh/My-New-Script.ps1` or `agent-ledger/scripts/My-New-Script.ps1`). Ensure the script is modular, handles parameters correctly, and fails gracefully with non-zero exit codes on error.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Register the command">
    Open the registry file `C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\pwsh\VaultWares CLI\vw-commands.ps1` and add a new entry to the `$Commands` hashtable:

    ```powershell
    "my-new-command" = @{
        ScriptPath  = "$RepoRoot\path\to\your\My-New-Script.ps1"
        Description = "Brief description of the tool's behavior."
        Category    = "Workspace Management" # Or another category
    }
    ```
  </Step>

  <Step title="Test the integration">
    Verify the command appears correctly in the CLI help menu and runs successfully:

    ```bash
    # Show CLI help to check categorization
    vw --help

    # Test run
    vw my-new-command --parameter value
    ```
  </Step>

  <Step title="Document the command">
    Update this document (`docs-content/operations/command-line-tool.mdx`) under the appropriate category section to keep developer resources synced.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Info>
  Always verify that the target script works inside non-interactive shells and does not wait on user input unless explicitly prompt-driven.
</Info>

QC.body:

## Protocole d'ajout

Pour ÃĐtendre l'interface en ligne de commande globale `vw` avec un nouveau script, suivez ces ÃĐtapes :

<Steps>
  <Step title="Placer le script dans l'espace de travail">
    Enregistrez le script dans le dÃĐpÃīt appropriÃĐ (par ex. `vault-explorer/scripts/pwsh/Mon-Nouveau-Script.ps1`). Assurez-vous que le script est modulaire, gÃĻre correctement ses paramÃĻtres, et retourne un code de sortie non nul en cas d'erreur.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Enregistrer la commande">
    Ouvrez le fichier de registre `C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\pwsh\VaultWares CLI\vw-commands.ps1` et ajoutez une entrÃĐe Ã  la table `$Commands` :

    ```powershell
    "mon-nouveau-script" = @{
        ScriptPath  = "$RepoRoot\chemin\vers\Mon-Nouveau-Script.ps1"
        Description = "BrÃĻve description du comportement du script."
        Category    = "Workspace Management" # Ou une autre catÃĐgorie
    }
    ```
  </Step>

  <Step title="Tester l'intÃĐgration">
    VÃĐrifiez que la commande apparaÃŪt correctement dans l'aide et s'exÃĐcute avec succÃĻs :

    ```bash
    # Affiche l'aide pour vÃĐrifier la catÃĐgorie
    vw --help

    # ExÃĐcute la commande de test
    vw mon-nouveau-script --paramÃĻtre valeur
    ```
  </Step>

  <Step title="Mettre Ã  jour la documentation">
    Ajoutez la commande dans ce document (`docs-content/operations/command-line-tool.mdx` et `command-line-tool-QC.mdx`) dans la section appropriÃĐe.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Info>
  VÃĐrifiez toujours que le script cible fonctionne dans des shells non-interactifs et ne requiert pas d'intervention utilisateur non spÃĐcifiÃĐe.
</Info>
